title: Proof receipt
description: One EXECUTION receipt per line in .agent-proof/<run>/ledger.jsonl. EXECUTION receipts record checks re-run by capture and always use disposition=reexecuted. CLAIM/VERDICT records (asserted/unparsed evidence) live in a SEPARATE file and follow claim.schema.json — structural separation, so an asserted claim can never be rendered as a re-executed check. As defense in depth, assert, verify, and stop-gate reject any schema_version>=2 line here whose disposition is not reexecuted.

label.description: Human-chosen name for the check, e.g. "test", "build", "curl-health".

command.description: The exact command that was run.

exit_code.description: The command's own exit code. 0 = passing; any non-zero fails the gate.

sha256.description: SHA-256 of the captured stdout+stderr log — the tamper-evident fingerprint of what actually happened.

log.description: Repo-relative path to the captured output log.

epoch.description: Unix epoch seconds at capture time. The stop-gate uses this (not file mtime) to judge freshness, so a stale receipt can't be made fresh with `touch`.

session.description: Agent session id when the harness exports AGENT_DONE_SESSION; empty otherwise.

commit.description: Full 40-char git HEAD SHA at capture time (empty outside a git repo). Binds the receipt to the source it verified, so the gate can detect a proof captured against different code.

tree.description: Git tree SHA of HEAD (git rev-parse HEAD^{tree}) at capture time; empty outside a git repo.

dirty.description: Whether the working tree had uncommitted changes (git status --porcelain non-empty) at capture time.

schema_version.description: Receipt format version. 1 introduced provenance (ci, ref); 2 introduced the evidence envelope (identity, environment, and disposition). Absent on receipts captured before v0.10.0; readers treat absence as version 0.

ci.description: Advisory, self-reported: true when a CI env var (GITHUB_ACTIONS or CI) was set at capture time. It is NOT an authenticity signal — anyone can set CI=1 locally. Trust that a check was CI-verified comes from the Action re-running it (mode: verify), not from this field.

ref.description: The git ref under test at capture time (GITHUB_REF); empty outside CI. Records which branch/PR the CI verification ran against.

repo.description: The git remote.origin.url at capture time; empty outside a git repository or when no origin remote is configured.

subject.description: The HEAD commit subject at capture time; empty outside a git repository.

producer.description: The capture engine and version that produced the receipt, e.g. done-gate.sh@0.11.0.

verifier.description: The verifier identity supplied through AGENT_DONE_VERIFIER; empty when capture was invoked directly.

host_os.description: Canonical host operating-system identity, shared by both engines: linux, darwin, windows, or unknown. Git Bash (uname MINGW*/MSYS*) resolves to windows so a receipt is engine-portable on the same machine.

disposition.description: How the evidence was handled. In an EXECUTION receipt (this ledger) it is ALWAYS "reexecuted" — capture ran the recorded command. The asserted and unparsed dispositions belong to CLAIM/VERDICT records, which live in a separate file and follow claim.schema.json; they are NOT execution proof, and every reader (assert, verify, stop-gate) rejects a schema_version>=2 ledger line whose disposition is not reexecuted.
